summary: Decompiled several item functions for Unown, Butterfree, Tingle, and Goldeen-related item files, while exposing additional item/attribute struct fields in itCommonItems.h and updating two headers from UNK signatures to typed Item_GObj* prototypes. Bot report confirms 8 new 100% function matches and one partial improvement for itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll.

key_files.role: Split padding in item variable and attribute structs to expose typed fields used by newly decompiled item functions.
Decompiled it_802CE710 and itUnknown_UnkMotion0_Anim; added stage include for blast zone checks.
Decompiled itPatapata_UnkMotion4_Anim and it_802E15B0; added includes for Nokonoko, lb_00B0, jobj, and camera helpers.
Decompiled it_802EBE5C, itTincle_UnkMotion7_Anim, it_802EC4D0, and it_802EC9E8; added baselib random include.
Replaced placeholder for itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll with C implementation using collision/transition helpers and PAD_STACK.
Updated it_802E15B0 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void it_802E15B0(Item_GObj*).
Updated it_802CE8D0 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void it_802CE8D0(Item_GObj*).

what_changed: Added C bodies for item functions previously represented by decomp placeholders in itunknown.c, itpatapata.c, ittincle.c, and itoldottosea.c.
Exposed fields in itTincle_ItemVars, itTincleAttributes, itPatapata_ItemVars, itPatapataAttributes, and itUnknownAttributes by replacing padding with typed members at known offsets.
Used item union arms such as ip->xDD4_itemVar.tincle, patapata, unknown, and nokonoko instead of raw pointer arithmetic in the diff excerpt.
Updated function prototypes in itpatapata.h and itunknown.h after bodies established concrete signatures.
Added required includes for helpers used by the new bodies, including baselib/random.h, baselib/jobj.h, gr/stage.h, lb_00B0.h, cm/camera.h, and itnokonoko.h.
Bot report recorded 8 new 100% matches: it_802CE710, itUnknown_UnkMotion0_Anim, itPatapata_UnkMotion4_Anim, it_802E15B0, it_802EBE5C, itTincle_UnkMotion7_Anim, it_802EC4D0, and it_802EC9E8.
Bot report recorded itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll as improved to 96.47%, which conflicts with the PR body claim that all functions were 100% matched.

smart_moves.text: Struct padding was split only where new functions needed typed access, keeping the changes tied to concrete item behavior rather than broad type cleanup.
Header prototypes were updated from unknown signatures after corresponding call shapes were visible in decompiled code.
The Tingle ECB handling copies ip->xDD4_itemVar.tincle.x68 into a local itECB before conditionally scaling fields and passing its address onward, avoiding raw offset manipulation.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When decompiling item functions, split item-var and attribute padding into narrow typed fields as the new C bodies prove offsets, rather than accessing item data through raw pointer arithmetic.
Recovered function bodies should drive cleanup of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes in the owning item headers.
PR body verification can disagree with automated match reports; preserve both and avoid treating a claimed 100% match as accepted when bot output reports only a partial improvement.

naming_conventions.text: Newly exposed struct members retained offset-style names such as x20, x40, x44, and x68 when semantic meaning was not established by the slice.

Locals use short role names visible in existing project style, including ip for Item*, gobj for Item_GObj*, jobj for HSD_JObj*, attrs/data for special attributes, and new_gobj/new_ip for spawned item state.
Address-style function names such as it_802E15B0 and it_802EC4D0 were preserved where the slice does not prove semantic names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK(8) was retained in itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll, itTincle_UnkMotion7_Anim, and it_802EC4D0 to affect stack/codegen shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded standards say PAD_STACK needs explicit verifier evidence; PR body claims all 100%, but bot report shows oldottosea only 96.47%.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll and HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll for Butterfree bone visibility toggling instead of expanded JObj flag code.
Used switch with grouped cases 6 through 11 in it_802EC9E8 to conditionally scale ECB fields.
Used Stage_GetBlastZone* helper calls in itUnknown_UnkMotion0_Anim to express boundary checks around item position.
Used HSD_Randi for random joint selection and random Tingle timer/range setup.

follow_up_queries: item headers UNK_RET UNK_PARAMS Item_GObj prototype cleanup

notes: Diff and changed-file metadata strongly support file changes, symbols touched, struct padding splits, includes, and header signature updates.
There are no review comments in the loaded slice, so review_feedback is empty.

Verification evidence is mixed: PR body claims all functions are 100% matched via objdiff and main.dol SHA1 OK, but bot report records 8 new 100% matches and itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll only improved to 96.47%.
No tool lookup was needed because loaded PR evidence included metadata, diff, changed files, PR body, and bot report.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Item decomp can expose typed item-var fields from padding when new function bodies prove offsets
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2290 split padding in itTincle_ItemVars, itTincleAttributes, itPatapata_ItemVars, itPatapataAttributes, and itUnknownAttributes to support newly decompiled item functions, with the C bodies using typed item union arms instead of raw offset access.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recovered item function bodies should clean owning header prototypes
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2290 changed it_802E15B0 and it_802CE8D0 declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void Item_GObj* prototypes after related item code established call shapes.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat PR body match claims cautiously when bot report disagrees
source_update_candidates.text: For PR intake and verification ledgers, preserve automated report discrepancies instead of promoting a PR-body claim of full matching. In PR #2290 the body says all functions matched 100%, while the bot report lists itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll as 96.47%.
source_update_candidates.reason: This may refine verification-ledger practice, but it should be curator-reviewed before becoming a global standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Item Pokemon files share item-var and attribute struct definitions in itCommonItems.h
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2290 modified item implementations under src/melee/it/items and exposed corresponding fields in src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h for Tingle, Patapata, and Unknown item data.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path relationship for future item decomp work, but source-owned path facts require curator review.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's blanket 'all functions 100% match' claim as-is because the bot report contradicts it for itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll.
Do not infer semantic names for xNN struct fields; the slice only supports offset-style fields.
Do not treat PAD_STACK use here as generally acceptable without narrow objdiff evidence, especially because one PAD_STACK function is reported below 100% by the bot.
